cards.front: How is the mass of one mole of a substance in grams related to its relative formula mass?
cards.back: It is numerically equal to the substance's relative formula mass.
cards.explanation: For a substance, the numerical value of the mass of one mole in grams equals its relative formula mass.

choices.text: It is numerically equal to the substance's relative formula mass.
choices.feedback: Correct. The mass of one mole in grams is numerically equal to the relative formula mass.

choices.text: It is numerically equal to the relative formula mass when measured in kilograms.
choices.feedback: The numerical equality applies when the mass of one mole is measured in grams, not kilograms.

choices.text: It is the same for every substance because every mole contains the same number of particles.
choices.feedback: Moles contain the same number of stated particles, but the mass of one mole in grams is tied to the substance's relative formula mass.

cards.sourceExcerpt:
The mass of one mole of a substance in grams is numerically equal
to its relative formula mass.
One mole of a substance contains the same number of the stated
particles, atoms, molecules or ions as one mole of any other
substance.
